description: GetSnowflakeOAuthURL — composes Snowflake OAuth authorize URL.
notes: Separate service namespace: `textql.rpc.public.snowflake_oauth.SnowflakeOAuthService` (NOT under `connector`). Discovered during the Snowflake OAuth (SSO) CreateConnector flow.

Thin helper — composes the authorize URL server-side. Caller supplies `state` (UUID), `role` (used to build `scope=session:role:<ROLE>`), `clientId`, and `accountUrl`. Server appends `access_type=offline`, `redirect_uri=https://app.textql.com/auth/snowflake/callback`, `response_type=code`.

Callback lands at `https://app.textql.com/auth/snowflake/callback` — that endpoint exchanges the auth code for a refresh token and associates it with the connector. Not captured here (would require a real Snowflake Security Integration to observe end-to-end).
`accountUrl` format: `https://<LOCATOR>.snowflakecomputing.com` (no trailing slash, uppercase locator). The webapp uppercases the locator before sending.

CLI implication: an `ana connector create snowflake oauth-sso` command can create the TextQL row headlessly (CreateConnector returns 200 without a refresh token), but the connector is not usable for queries until a human finishes the OAuth handshake in a browser. The ana CLI cannot complete the handshake itself because the redirect URI is hard-coded to app.textql.com, not localhost.
